title: Bogenhausen

(German pronunciation: [[ˈboːɡn̩haʊzn̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Bogenhausen.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Bogenhausen.wav"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Bognhausn*) is the 13th borough of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). It is the geographically the third largest borough of Munich and comprises the city's north-eastern quarter, reaching from the Isar on the eastern side of the [Englischer Garten](/wiki/Englischer_Garten "Englischer Garten") to the city limits, bordering on [Unterföhring](/wiki/Unterf%C3%B6hring "Unterföhring") to the north, [Aschheim](/wiki/Aschheim "Aschheim") to the east and the [Haidhausen](/wiki/Haidhausen_(Munich) "Haidhausen (Munich)") borough to the south. In 1805 the [Treaty of Bogenhausen](/wiki/Treaty_of_Bogenhausen "Treaty of Bogenhausen") was signed which allied Bavaria to [Napoleonic France](/wiki/Napoleonic_France "Napoleonic France").

is the oldest part of Bogenhausen and is located between the river Isar to the west, the [Prinzregentenstraße](/wiki/Prinzregentenstrasse_(Munich) "Prinzregentenstrasse (Munich)") to the south and the [Mittlerer Ring](/wiki/Mittlerer_Ring "Mittlerer Ring") to the east and north. Alt-Bogenhausen is one of Munich's most desirable residential districts and has some of the highest quality housing in town which comes with the highest rental prices in Germany.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The borough's main artery is *[Ismaninger Straße](/wiki/Ismaninger_Stra%C3%9Fe "Ismaninger Straße")*, connecting Prinzregentenstraße to the south with Mittlerer Ring in the north at [Effnerplatz](/w/index.php?title=Effnerplatz&action=edit&redlink=1 "Effnerplatz (page does not exist)"). The district is serviced by the tram lines 16 and 18 as well as the bus lines 54, 154, 100, 187, 188 and 189. The nearest [Munich U-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_U-Bahn "Munich U-Bahn") stations are [Prinzregentenplatz](/wiki/Prinzregentenplatz_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Prinzregentenplatz (Munich U-Bahn)") to the south-east and [Böhmerwaldplatz](/wiki/B%C3%B6hmerwaldplatz_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Böhmerwaldplatz (Munich U-Bahn)") and [Richard-Strauss-Straße](/wiki/Richard-Strauss-Stra%C3%9Fe_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Richard-Strauss-Straße (Munich U-Bahn)") to the east.

is a quiet, secretive and highly exclusive residential area north of Alt-Bogenhausen, enclosed between the river Isar to the west and the *Isarhochufer* (lit. Isar cliff line). It is considered the most expensive and classy of Munich's numerous upscale residential districts, being the preferred living environment of the more reclusive commercial and political elite of the city (in contrast to film and sports stars, who either prefer the more urban districts of the inner city or the suburb of [Grünwald](/wiki/Gr%C3%BCnwald,_Bavaria "Grünwald, Bavaria")). The whole area lies hidden between lush vegetation at the foot of the Isarhochufer, making it practically invisible from the outside - even from surrounding vantage points. The housing mostly consists of early-20th century villas as well as modern villas, built after [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"). While most of the district is free of car traffic, the *[Isarring](/wiki/Isarring "Isarring")* highway, part of the Mittlerer Ring road system, divides the district into a northern and a southern part, leading to complaints by neighbours due to extensive car noise. The district is serviced by the bus line 187.

and **Oberföhring** are two residential quarters north of Alt-Bogenhausen and up the slope from Herzogpark, straddling *Oberföhringer Straße*, the districts' main traffic artery. The housing mostly consists of apartment buildings, affording a great view across the city. Oberföhring became part of Munich on 1 July 1913.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

is located on the river Isar and is a lush enclave of green. It is named after the *St. Emmeram Chapel*, which in turn is named after [Otloh of St. Emmeram](/wiki/Otloh_of_St._Emmeram "Otloh of St. Emmeram").

is located east of the S8 train line. The district mostly consists of detached and semi-detached houses and has retained some of its rural character. It is serviced by the [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/M%C3%BCnchen_S8.svg/40px-M%C3%BCnchen_S8.svg.png)](/wiki/S8_(Munich) "S8 (Munich)") at the [Johanneskirchen S-Bahn station](/wiki/M%C3%BCnchen-Johanneskirchen_station "München-Johanneskirchen station").

["Bevölkerung, Fläche und Bevölkerungsdichte am 31.12.2023 in den Stadtbezirken"](https://stadt.muenchen.de/dam/jcr:370fe775-89d2-4a81-b7ca-464ddb514549/jt240102.pdf) (PDF) (in German). Retrieved 2024-08-19.

["Best Neighborhoods in Munich - Overview & Comparison | Moving to Munich"](https://web.archive.org/web/20151124160317/http://www.moving-to-munich.com/best-neighborhoods-in-munich/) (in German). 2015-06-09. Archived from the original on November 24, 2015. Retrieved 2021-04-23.

**Bogenhausen** (German pronunciation: [[ˈboːɡn̩haʊzn̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Bogenhausen.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Bogenhausen.wav"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Bognhausn*) is the 13th borough of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). It is the geographically the third largest borough of Munich and comprises the city's north-eastern quarter, reaching from the Isar on the eastern side of the [Englischer Garten](/wiki/Englischer_Garten "Englischer Garten") to the city limits, bordering on [Unterföhring](/wiki/Unterf%C3%B6hring "Unterföhring") to the north, [Aschheim](/wiki/Aschheim "Aschheim") to the east and the [Haidhausen](/wiki/Haidhausen_(Munich) "Haidhausen (Munich)") borough to the south. In 1805 the [Treaty of Bogenhausen](/wiki/Treaty_of_Bogenhausen "Treaty of Bogenhausen") was signed which allied Bavaria to [Napoleonic France](/wiki/Napoleonic_France "Napoleonic France").

## Sub-divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bogenhausen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Sub-divisions")]

### Alt-Bogenhausen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bogenhausen&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Alt-Bogenhausen")]

**Alt-Bogenhausen** is the oldest part of Bogenhausen and is located between the river Isar to the west, the [Prinzregentenstraße](/wiki/Prinzregentenstrasse_(Munich) "Prinzregentenstrasse (Munich)") to the south and the [Mittlerer Ring](/wiki/Mittlerer_Ring "Mittlerer Ring") to the east and north. Alt-Bogenhausen is one of Munich's most desirable residential districts and has some of the highest quality housing in town which comes with the highest rental prices in Germany.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The borough's main artery is *[Ismaninger Straße](/wiki/Ismaninger_Stra%C3%9Fe "Ismaninger Straße")*, connecting Prinzregentenstraße to the south with Mittlerer Ring in the north at [Effnerplatz](/w/index.php?title=Effnerplatz&action=edit&redlink=1 "Effnerplatz (page does not exist)"). The district is serviced by the tram lines 16 and 18 as well as the bus lines 54, 154, 100, 187, 188 and 189. The nearest [Munich U-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_U-Bahn "Munich U-Bahn") stations are [Prinzregentenplatz](/wiki/Prinzregentenplatz_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Prinzregentenplatz (Munich U-Bahn)") to the south-east and [Böhmerwaldplatz](/wiki/B%C3%B6hmerwaldplatz_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Böhmerwaldplatz (Munich U-Bahn)") and [Richard-Strauss-Straße](/wiki/Richard-Strauss-Stra%C3%9Fe_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Richard-Strauss-Straße (Munich U-Bahn)") to the east.

Many well known and wealthy Persons and families have houses in Alt-Bogenhausen for example Käfers (Feinkost Käfer), [Sedelmayrs](/wiki/Sedlmayr "Sedlmayr"), [Burdas](/wiki/Hubert_Burda "Hubert Burda"), [Samwers](/w/index.php?title=Alexander_Samwer&action=edit&redlink=1 "Alexander Samwer (page does not exist)"), [Quanndts](/wiki/Stefan_Quandt "Stefan Quandt"), [Strauss](/wiki/Monika_Hohlmeier "Monika Hohlmeier") and [Roland Berger](/wiki/Roland_Berger "Roland Berger").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Hypo-Haus.JPG/250px-Hypo-Haus.JPG)](/wiki/File:Hypo-Haus.JPG)

[HVB Tower](/wiki/Hypo-Haus "Hypo-Haus") at [Arabellapark](/wiki/Arabellapark "Arabellapark")

### Herzogpark

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bogenhausen&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Herzogpark")]

**Herzogpark** is a quiet, secretive and highly exclusive residential area north of Alt-Bogenhausen, enclosed between the river Isar to the west and the *Isarhochufer* (lit. Isar cliff line). It is considered the most expensive and classy of Munich's numerous upscale residential districts, being the preferred living environment of the more reclusive commercial and political elite of the city (in contrast to film and sports stars, who either prefer the more urban districts of the inner city or the suburb of [Grünwald](/wiki/Gr%C3%BCnwald,_Bavaria "Grünwald, Bavaria")). The whole area lies hidden between lush vegetation at the foot of the Isarhochufer, making it practically invisible from the outside - even from surrounding vantage points. The housing mostly consists of early-20th century villas as well as modern villas, built after [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"). While most of the district is free of car traffic, the *[Isarring](/wiki/Isarring "Isarring")* highway, part of the Mittlerer Ring road system, divides the district into a northern and a southern part, leading to complaints by neighbours due to extensive car noise. The district is serviced by the bus line 187.

### Priel, Oberföhring and St. Emmeram

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bogenhausen&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Priel, Oberföhring and St. Emmeram")]

**Priel** and **Oberföhring** are two residential quarters north of Alt-Bogenhausen and up the slope from Herzogpark, straddling *Oberföhringer Straße*, the districts' main traffic artery. The housing mostly consists of apartment buildings, affording a great view across the city. Oberföhring became part of Munich on 1 July 1913.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

**St. Emmeram** is located on the river Isar and is a lush enclave of green. It is named after the *St. Emmeram Chapel*, which in turn is named after [Otloh of St. Emmeram](/wiki/Otloh_of_St._Emmeram "Otloh of St. Emmeram").

### Johanneskirchen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bogenhausen&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Johanneskirchen")]

**Johanneskirchen** is located east of the S8 train line. The district mostly consists of detached and semi-detached houses and has retained some of its rural character. It is serviced by the [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/M%C3%BCnchen_S8.svg/40px-M%C3%BCnchen_S8.svg.png)](/wiki/S8_(Munich) "S8 (Munich)") at the [Johanneskirchen S-Bahn station](/wiki/M%C3%BCnchen-Johanneskirchen_station "München-Johanneskirchen station").

### Other quarters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bogenhausen&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Other quarters")]

The Bogenhausen borough also includes Arabellapark, Cosimapark, and Johanneskirchen to the north and north-east, as well as [Englschalking](/wiki/Englschalking "Englschalking"), [Denning](/wiki/Denning_(Munich) "Denning (Munich)"), Zamdorf and Daglfing to the east. Especially Denning and Englschalking have developed into very idyllic and affluent suburban residential districts, making them extremely popular with well-off families who increasingly build new houses and villas there. Parts of Englschalking and almost the whole of Daglfing (which lies just outside Munich's official city limits, bordering Denning and Zamdorf), have retained a distinctive rural character, still featuring old farm buildings and fields. The contrast between these rural and the more modern suburban areas becomes apparent as soon as one crosses the [Munich S-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_S-Bahn "Munich S-Bahn") rails of Line [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/M%C3%BCnchen_S8.svg/40px-M%C3%BCnchen_S8.svg.png)](/wiki/S8_(Munich) "S8 (Munich)"), which practically coincides with Munich's eastern border in this area.

The other parts of the Stadtbezirk are a relatively newly developed mixture of commercial and residential areas. Even Arabellapark, which became Munich's first true cluster of high-rise buildings in the 80's, and Cosimapark, a post-World War II housing estate, were predominantly green fields until the 60's, but have been rapidly developed since then. You can find a mixture of offices, restaurants, shops, sports facilities and schools there, alternating with typical German apartment buildings from the 60's and 70's right up to newly built offerings, older and newer detached and semi-detached houses, villas, and quite a few authentic farm houses that survived modern developments. Additionally, multitudes of parks and playgrounds dot the whole area.
